questions.questionText:
There are 3 sections in a question paper and
each section contains 5 questions. A candidate
has to answer a total of 5 questions, choosing
at least one question from each section. Then
the number of ways, in which the candidate
can choose the questions, is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
A galvanometer is used in laboratory for
detecting the null point in electrical
experiments. If, on passing a current of 6 mA it
produces a deflection of 2<sup>o</sup>, its figure of merit
is close to :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Consider the complex ions,
trans-[Co(en)2Cl2]+ (A) and
cis-[Co(en)2Cl2]+ (B).
The correct statement regarding them is :
options: (A) both (A) and (B) can be optically active.
(B) both (A) and (B) can not be optically active.

(C) (A) can not be optically active, but (B) can
be optically active.

(D) (A) can be optically active, but (B) can not
be optically active.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
The variation of molar conductivity with concentration of an electrolyte (X) in aqueous solution is shown in the given figure.
The electrolyte X is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
If the sum of the second, third and fourth terms
of a positive term G.P. is 3 and the sum of its
sixth, seventh and eighth terms is 243, then the
sum of the first 50 terms of this G.P. is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
If the mean and the standard deviation of the
data 3, 5, 7, a, b are 5 and 2 respectively, then a and b are the roots of the equation :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
The acceleration due to gravity on the earth’s
surface at the poles is g and angular velocity of
the earth about the axis passing through the
pole is $$\omega $$. An object is weighed at the equator
and at a height h above the poles by using a
spring balance. If the weights are found to be
same, then h is (h << R, where R is the radius
of the earth)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: In the circuit shown, charge on the 5 $$\mu $$F capacitor is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: An infinitely long, straight wire carrying current I, one side opened rectangular loop and a conductor C with a sliding connector are located in the same plane, as shown, in the figure. The connector has length $$l$$ and resistance R. It slides to the right with a velocity v. The resistance of the conductor and the self inductance of the loop are negligible. The induced current in the loop, as a function of separation r, between the connector and the straight wire is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
In an experiment to verify Stokes law, a small spherical ball of radius r and density $$\rho $$ falls under gravity through a distance h in air before entering a tank of water. If the terminal velocity of the ball inside water is same as its velocity just before entering the water surface, then the value of h is proportional to :
(ignore viscosity of air)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The velocity (v) and time (t) graph of a body in a straight line motion is shown in the figure. The point S is at 4.333 seconds. The total distance covered by the body in 6 s is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Two coherent sources of sound, S1 and S2, produce sound waves of the same wavelength, $$\lambda $$ = 1 m, in phase. S1 and S2 are placed 1.5 m apart (see fig). A listener, located at L, directly in front of S2 finds that the intensity is at a minimum
when he is 2 m away from S2. The listener moves away from S1, keeping his distance from S2 fixed. The adjacent maximum of intensity is observed when the listener is at a distance d from S1. Then, d is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
A parallel plate capacitor has plate of length
'l', width ‘w’ and separation of plates is ‘d’. It
is connected to a battery of emf V. A dielectric
slab of the same thickness ‘d’ and of dielectric
constant k = 4 is being inserted between the
plates of the capacitor. At what length of the
slab inside plates, will the energy stored in the
capacitor be two times the initial energy
stored?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
In an adiabatic process, the density of a
diatomic gas becomes 32 times its initial value.
The final pressure of the gas is found to be n
times the initial pressure. The value of n is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
An iron rod of volume 10<sup>–3</sup> m<sup>3</sup> and relative
permeability 1000 is placed as core in a
solenoid with 10 turns/cm. If a current of 0.5 A
is passed through the solenoid, then the
magnetic moment of the rod will be :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
A radioactive nucleus decays by two different
processes. The half life for the first process is
10 s and that for the second is 100 s. The
effective half life of the nucleus is close to :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
A driver in a car, approaching a vertical wall
notices that the frequency of his car horn, has
changed from 440 Hz to 480 Hz, when it gets
reflected from the wall. If the speed of sound in
air is 345 m/s, then the speed of the car is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: In the circuit, given in the figure currents in different branches and value of one resistor are shown. Then potential at point B with respect to the point A is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Two Zener diodes (A and B) having breakdown voltages of 6 V and 4 V respectively, are connected as shown in the circuit below. The output voltage V0 variation with input voltage linearly increasing with time, is given by :
(Vinput = 0 V at t = 0)
(figures are qualitative)
options: (A)
(B)
(C)
(D)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
The quantities x = $${1 \over {\sqrt {{\mu _0}{\varepsilon _0}} }}$$, y = $${E \over B}$$ and z = $${l \over {CR}}$$ are
defined where C-capacitance, R-Resistance, l-length, E-Electric field, B-magnetic field and $${{\varepsilon _0}}$$, $${{\mu _0}}$$, - free space permittivity and permeability respectively. Then :
options: (A) Only y and z have the same dimension
(B) x, y and z have the same dimension
(C) Only x and y have the same dimension
(D) Only x and z have the same dimension
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Ten charges are placed on the circumference of a circle of radius R with constant angular separation between successive charges. Alternate charges 1, 3, 5, 7, 9 have charge (+q) each, while 2, 4, 6, 8, 10 have charge (–q) each. The potential V and the electric field E at the centre of the circle are respectively.
(Take V = 0 at infinity)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
The correct match between the entries in column I and column II are :

I II
Radiation Wavelength
(a) Microwave (i) 100 m
(b) Gamma rays (ii) 10–15 m
(c) A.M. radio waves (iii) 10–10 m
(d) X-rays (iv) 10–3 m
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
A body of mass 2 kg is driven by an engine
delivering a constant power of 1 J/s. The body
starts from rest and moves in a straight line.
After 9 seconds, the body has moved a
distance (in m) _______.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
A spaceship in space sweeps stationary interplanetary dust. As a result, its mass
increases at a rate $${{dM\left( t \right)} \over {dt}}$$ = bv2(t), where v(t) is its instantaneous velocity. The instantaneous acceleration of the satellite is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
A ring is hung on a nail. It can oscillate, without slipping or sliding
(i) in its plane with a time period T1 and,
(ii) back and forth in a direction perpendicular to its plane,
with a period T2. The ratio $${{{T_1}} \over {{T_2}}}$$ will be :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
The surface of a metal is illuminated alternately
with photons of energies E<sub>1</sub> = 4 eV and E<sub>2</sub> = 2.5 eV
respectively. The ratio of maximum speeds of the
photoelectrons emitted in the two cases is 2. The
work function of the metal in (eV) is _____.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Two different wires having lengths L<sub>1</sub> and L<sub>2</sub>,
and respective temperature coefficient of linear
expansion $$\alpha $$<sub>1</sub> and $$\alpha $$<sub>2</sub>, are joined end-to-end.
Then the effective temperature coefficient of
linear expansion is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
A prism of angle A = 1o has a refractive index $$\mu $$ = 1.5. A good estimate for the minimum angle of deviation (in degrees) is close to $${N \over {10}}$$.
Value of N is ____.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Nitrogen gas is at 300oC temperature. The temperature (in K) at which the rms speed of a H2 molecule would be equal to the rms speed of a nitrogen molecule, is _______.
(Molar mass of N2 gas 28 g).
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The one that is NOT suitable for the removal of permanent hardness of water is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A thin rod of mass 0.9 kg and length 1 m is suspended, at rest, from one end so that it can freely oscillate in the vertical plane. A particle of move 0.1 kg moving in a straight line with velocity 80 m/s hits the rod at its bottom most point and sticks to it (see figure). The angular speed (in rad/s) of the rod immediately after the collision will be _________.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
An element crystallises in a face-centred cubic (fcc) unit cell with cell edge $$a$$. The distance
between the centres of two nearest octahedral voids in the crystal lattice is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
The correct order of the ionic radii of
O2–, N3–, F– , Mg2+, Na+ and Al3+ is :
options:
(A) N<sup>3–</sup> < O<sup>2–</sup> < F<sup>–</sup>
 < Na<sup>+</sup> < Mg<sup>2+</sup> < Al<sup>3+</sup>

(B) N<sup>3–</sup> < F<sup>–</sup>
 < O<sup>2–</sup> < Mg<sup>2+</sup> < Na<sup>+</sup> < Al<sup>3+</sup>

(C) Al<sup>3+</sup> < Na<sup>+</sup> < Mg<sup>2+</sup> < O<sup>2–</sup> < F<sup>–</sup>
 < N<sup>3–</sup>

(D) Al<sup>3+</sup> < Mg<sup>2+</sup> < Na<sup>+</sup> < F<sup>–</sup>
 < O<sup>2–</sup> < N<sup>3–</sup>
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The increasing order of boiling points of the following compounds is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Lattice enthalpy and enthalpy of solution of NaCl are 788 kJ mol–1, and 4 kJ mol–1, respectively.
The hydration enthalpy of NaCl is :

Reaction of ammonia with excess Cl<sub>2</sub>
 gives :

(B)
(C)

The rate constant (k) of a reaction is measured at differenct temperatures (T), and the data are plotted in the given figure. The activation energy of the reaction in kJ mol–1 is :
(R is gas constant)

questions.questionText: Boron and silicon of very high purity can be obtained through :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Adsorption of a gas follows Freundlich adsorption isotherm. If x is the mass of the gas adsorbed on mass
m of the adsorbent, the correct plot of $${x \over m}$$ versus p is :
options: (A)
(B)
(C)
(D)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The compound that has the largest H–M–H bond angle (M = N, O, S, C) is :

The major product formed in the following reaction is :
CH3CH = CHCH(CH3)2 $$\buildrel {HBr} \over \longrightarrow $$

questions.questionText: Among the following compounds, geometrical isomerism is exhibited by :
options: (A)
(B)
(C)
(D)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The correct statement about probability density (except at infinite distance from nucleus) is :
options: (A) It can be zero for 1s orbital
(B) It can be zero for 3p orbital
(C) It can never be zero for 2s orbital
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The following molecule acts as an :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
The number of chiral carbons present in
sucrose is _____.

questions.questionText: Hydrogen peroxide, in the pure state, is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
For a dimerization reaction,
2A(g) $$ \to $$ A2(g)
at 298 K, $$\Delta $$Uo = –20 kJ mol–1, $$\Delta $$So = –30 JK–1 mol–1,
then the $$\Delta $$Go will be _____ J.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
The volume, in mL, of 0.02 M K2Cr2O7 solution required to react with 0.288 g of ferrous oxalate in acidic medium is _______.
(Molar mass of Fe = 56 g mol–1)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Considering that $$\Delta $$0 > P, the magnetic moment
(in BM) of [Ru(H2O)6]2+ would be _________.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
For a reaction X + Y ⇌ 2Z , 1.0 mol of X, 1.5 mol
of Y and 0.5 mol of Z were taken in a 1 L vessel and
allowed to react. At equilibrium, the concentration
of Z was 1.0 mol L–1. The equilibrium constant of reaction
is $${x \over {15}}$$. The value of x is _________.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
The area (in sq. units) of the region

A = {(x, y) : (x – 1)[x] $$ \le $$ y $$ \le $$ 2$$\sqrt x $$, 0 $$ \le $$ x $$ \le $$ 2}, where [t]

denotes the greatest integer function, is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
The derivative of
$${\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {{{\sqrt {1 + {x^2}} - 1} \over x}} \right)$$ with
respect to $${\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {{{2x\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} } \over {1 - 2{x^2}}}} \right)$$ at x = $${1 \over 2}$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
If
$$\int {{{\cos \theta } \over {5 + 7\sin \theta - 2{{\cos }^2}\theta }}} d\theta $$ = A$${\log _e}\left| {B\left( \theta \right)} \right| + C$$,

where C is a constant of integration, then $${{{B\left( \theta \right)} \over A}}$$
can be :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
If the line y = mx + c is a common tangent to the hyperbola
$${{{x^2}} \over {100}} - {{{y^2}} \over {64}} = 1$$ and the circle x2 + y2 = 36, then which one of the following is true?
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
If L = sin2$$\left( {{\pi \over {16}}} \right)$$ - sin2$$\left( {{\pi \over {8}}} \right)$$ and
M = cos2$$\left( {{\pi \over {16}}} \right)$$ - sin2$$\left( {{\pi \over {8}}} \right)$$, then :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
If the sum of the first 20 terms of the series
$${\log _{\left( {{7^{1/2}}} \right)}}x + {\log _{\left( {{7^{1/3}}} \right)}}x + {\log _{\left( {{7^{1/4}}} \right)}}x + ...$$ is 460,
then x is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
If $$\alpha $$ and $$\beta $$ are the roots of the equation,
7x2 – 3x – 2 = 0, then the value of
$${\alpha \over {1 - {\alpha ^2}}} + {\beta \over {1 - {\beta ^2}}}$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
If the system of linear equations
x + y + 3z = 0
x + 3y + k2z = 0
3x + y + 3z = 0
has a non-zero solution (x, y, z) for some k $$ \in $$ R, then x + $$\left( {{y \over z}} \right)$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
If the length of the chord of the circle,
x2 + y2 = r2 (r > 0) along the line, y – 2x = 3 is r,
then r2 is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
If for some $$\alpha $$ $$ \in $$ R, the lines

L1 : $${{x + 1} \over 2} = {{y - 2} \over { - 1}} = {{z - 1} \over 1}$$ and

L2 : $${{x + 2} \over \alpha } = {{y + 1} \over {5 - \alpha }} = {{z + 1} \over 1}$$ are coplanar,

then the line L2 passes through the point :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
If x = 1 is a critical point of the function
f(x) = (3x2 + ax – 2 – a)ex , then :
options:
(A) x = 1 is a local maxima and x = $$ - {2 \over 3}$$ is a
local minima of f.
(B) x = 1 and x = $$ - {2 \over 3}$$ are local maxima of f.
(C) x = 1 and x = $$ - {2 \over 3}$$ are local minima of f.

(D) x = 1 is a local minima and x = $$ - {2 \over 3}$$ is a local
maxima of f.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
The statement
$$\left( {p \to \left( {q \to p} \right)} \right) \to \left( {p \to \left( {p \vee q} \right)} \right)$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: $$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} {{x\left( {{e^{\left( {\sqrt {1 + {x^2} + {x^4}}  - 1} \right)/x}} - 1} \right)} \over {\sqrt {1 + {x^2} + {x^4}}  - 1}}$$
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let y = y(x) be the solution of the differential equation

cosx$${{dy} \over {dx}}$$ + 2ysinx = sin2x, x $$ \in $$ $$\left( {0,{\pi \over 2}} \right)$$.

If y$$\left( {{\pi \over 3}} \right)$$ = 0, then y$$\left( {{\pi \over 4}} \right)$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The value of $${\left( {{{ - 1 + i\sqrt 3 } \over {1 - i}}} \right)^{30}}$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Which of the following points lies on the tangent to the curve

x4ey + 2$$\sqrt {y + 1} $$ = 3 at the point (1, 0)?
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
If a + x = b + y = c + z + 1, where a, b, c, x, y, z
are non-zero distinct real numbers, then
$$\left| {\matrix{ x & {a + y} & {x + a} \cr y & {b + y} & {y + b} \cr z & {c + y} & {z + c} \cr } } \right|$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let the vectors $$\overrightarrow a $$, $$\overrightarrow b $$, $$\overrightarrow c $$ be such that
$$\left| {\overrightarrow a } \right| = 2$$, $$\left| {\overrightarrow b } \right| = 4$$ and $$\left| {\overrightarrow c } \right| = 4$$. If the projection of
$$\overrightarrow b $$ on $$\overrightarrow a $$ is equal to the projection of $$\overrightarrow c $$ on $$\overrightarrow a $$
and $$\overrightarrow b $$ is perpendicular to $$\overrightarrow c $$, then the value of
$$\left| {\overrightarrow a + \vec b - \overrightarrow c } \right|$$ is ___________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
The coefficient of x4 in the expansion of
(1 + x + x2 + x3)6 in powers of x, is ______.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
In a bombing attack, there is 50% chance that
a bomb will hit the target. Atleast two
independent hits are required to destroy the
target completely. Then the minimum number
of bombs, that must be dropped to ensure that
there is at least 99% chance of completely
destroying the target, is __________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
If the lines x + y = a and x – y = b touch the
curve y = x2 – 3x + 2 at the points where the curve intersects the x-axis, then $${a \over b}$$ is equal to _______.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let A = {a, b, c} and B = {1, 2, 3, 4}. Then the number of elements in the set
C = {f : A $$ \to $$ B | 2 $$ \in $$ f(A) and f is not one-one} is ______.
questions.subject: Mathematics
